title: aeschynanthus dempoensis
intro: Aeschynanthus dempoensis, also known as Dempo toad-flax or Lipstick Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Borneo and is a popular ornamental plant due to its unique, bright red flowers and ability to thrive in low-light conditions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The leaves and flowers of Aeschynanthus dempoensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
